title: Remove obsolete Node tooling (package.json, package-lock.json, Dockerfile)
description: Once on deno kernel + flake-based env, the Node-era artifacts are dead weight: package.json, package-lock.json (npm deps now via `npm:` specifiers); Dockerfile (mybinder.org image built tslab + node). Decide whether to drop the mybinder binder entirely or rewrite it for deno. README has a mybinder badge to update either way.
close_reason: Removed package.json, package-lock.json, Dockerfile. Updated README to drop mybinder badge and document 'nix develop + jupyter lab' workflow. Pruned .gitignore to project-relevant sections.

title: Initialize Nix flake from basic template with deno + jupyter deps
description: Run `nix flake init -t github:keithschulze/nix-templates#basic` and extend the devShell with deno (for the jupyter kernel) and a jupyter frontend (jupyterlab via python). This becomes the entry point for working on the notebook.
close_reason: Flake initialized from basic template; deno + jupyter added to devShell; project-local Deno kernelspec auto-generated by shellHook

title: Switch notebook to deno jupyter kernel
description: Replace tslab (Node-based TS kernel) with deno's built-in jupyter kernel. Update folds.ipynb metadata to use the Deno kernel. With deno, npm packages are imported via `npm:` specifiers directly in cells, so package.json/node_modules become unnecessary.
close_reason: Notebook kernelspec switched to Deno (display_name=Deno, language=typescript). Stale tslab metadata and outputs cleared.

title: Migrate aws-sdk usage from v2 to v3 in folds.ipynb
description: Cells 25-32 use aws-sdk v2 API: `import AWS from 'aws-sdk'`, `new AWS.S3()`, `.listObjectsV2({...}).promise()`. Migrate to v3 modular client: `import { S3Client, ListObjectsV2Command } from 'npm:@aws-sdk/client-s3'` and `s3.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({...}))`.
close_reason: Cells 25, 26, 27, 32 migrated to @aws-sdk/client-s3 v3 with npm: specifiers: S3Client + ListObjectsV2Command + s3.send(). Verified with 'deno check'.
